data.title: FTSE Straits Times Index Extends Gains as Singapore PM Unveils Budget
SpaceX’s next Mars rocket could include Optimus robots and Grok — Musk
data.description:
Elon Musk plans to send a Tesla Bot, powered by xAI’s artificial intelligence model Grok, on SpaceX’s next Mars mission by the end of 2026.

On Feb. 18, Musk announced xAI’s latest AI model release, Grok 3, in a livestream on X. During a discussion, he revealed that most of SpaceX’s projects for Mars exploration are slated for around Q4 2026. 

He explained that the Earth-Mars transit window occurs every 26 months, making November 2026 the next ideal opportunity for rocket launches to the Red Planet.

Elon Musk prepares Grok-powered Tesla Bots for space exploration

Musk also said he may be sending a Tesla Bot and Grok on the Mars mission:

“If all goes well, SpaceX will send Starship rockets to Mars with Optimus robots and Grok.”

The AI model Grok 3, which xAI developed, recently outperformed rival AI systems from Google, OpenAI and DeepSeek in a blind evaluation conducted by Chatbot Arena, an open-source AI benchmarking platform.

The large language model (LLM) blind test allowed users to ask questions to two anonymous AI chatbots and rank them based on their responses. The tests have collectively recorded over a million community votes.

Musk and the xAI team claimed that the early Grok 3 model — codenamed chocolate — achieved a record milestone of 1400 score. “And it’s still climbing. So we have to keep updating it. It’s 1400 and climbing,” Musk said.

Grok 3 outperforms all AI models in benchmark test

LMArena also noted that the early Grok 3 model currently ranks first in all categories, including overall with style control, hard prompts and hard prompts with style control, coding, math, creative writing, instruction following, longer query and multi-turn.

On Feb. 12, an xAI engineer quit over an X post in which he had ranked Grok 3 lower than ChatGPT, sharing his personal opinion prior to the model’s release.

“I either had to delete the post quoted below or face being fired, DeKraker wrote, adding:

“After reviewing everything and thinking a lot, I’ve decided that I’m not going to delete the post -- which is very clearly a harmless personal opinion.”

data.title: Jingjiang Industrial Investment Takes Controlling Stake in Guanfu Holding
data.description:
State-owned Hubei Jingjiang Industrial Investment Group has acquired a controlling stake in Guanfu Holding , according to a Tuesday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

The acquisition was triggered by Guanfu's cancellation of repurchased shares, which resulted in a passive increase in Jingjiang's voting rights to over 30% of all issued shares.

Jingjiang Industrial is a subsidiary of Jingzhou City Urban Development Holding Group, which in turn is controlled by the Jingzhou Municipal People's Government State-owned Assets Supervision and Administration Commission.

data.title: Hollywood Bowl Group Launches GBP10 Million Share Buyback
data.description:
Hollywood Bowl Group launched a share buyback program of up to 10 million pounds sterling.

The British bowling operator will repurchase up to 17,208,385 shares until Jan. 29, 2026, according to a Tuesday filing.

Investec and Berenberg will manage the program, which aims to reduce the company's share capital.

data.title: UK 10-Year Gilt Yield Rises after Wages Data
data.description:
The UK’s 10-year government bond yield climbed to 4.556%, near its highest in two weeks, after data showed wages rising at their fastest pace since last April.

Salaries, excluding bonuses, increased by 5.9% in the last quarter of 2023, up from 5.6% in the previous period, signaling persistent inflation pressures.

Wages, including bonuses, grew by 6%, surpassing expectations.

Meanwhile, unemployment held steady at 4.4%, defying forecasts of a rise.

As a result, investors slightly reduced their expectations for Bank of England rate cuts this year, now anticipating 54 basis points of cuts instead of 57.

Traders are now awaiting inflation data on Wednesday for further clues.

Additionally, markets are factoring in the possibility that a Ukraine peace deal could lead to higher UK government spending.

data.title: Thailand's GDP Accelerates in Q4 2024
data.description:
Thailand's economy accelerated in the fourth quarter despite the trade risks the country faces.

The gross domestic product for the quarter between October and December climbed 3.2% year on year, according to Monday data from the National Economic and Social Development Council or NESDC.

The latest figure expanded from the previous quarter's 3% growth but missed estimates of a 3.9% rise predicted by analysts surveyed by Reuters.

For the entire of 2024, the Thai economy is expected to increase by 2.5% upon the expansion of private consumption expenditure and growth in the production sector.

The GDP this year is predicted to grow between 2.3% and 3.3% due to a rise in government expenditure, particularly investment spending, the ongoing expansion of private consumption, as well as the recovery of the tourism sector, and a rise in merchandise exports, the NESDC said.

However, the NESDC said the country should prioritize mitigating the impact of trade policy changes, especially the new tariffs being introduced by the US, as well as protect the manufacturing sector from dumping and unfair trade practices.

During a press conference, NESDC head Danucha Pichayanan said that Thailand's trade surplus with the US was at risk due to new levies that President Donald Trump is introducing, according to a Reuters report on Monday.

data.title: Russian rouble weakens as Russia-U.S. talks begin in Riyadh
data.description:
The Russian rouble weakened against the dollar on Tuesday as Russian and U.S. officials met in Saudi Arabia for the most significant talks to date between the two former Cold War foes on ending Moscow's war in Ukraine.

At 0807 GMT, the rouble was down 0.8% at 90.33 against the dollar on the Moscow Exchange, though still close to a five-month high reached last week.

It was also down 0.6% against China's yuan, the most traded foreign currency in Russia.

The Russian currency has gained about 20% against the dollar this year, mostly on expectations of easing geopolitical tensions between Russia and the West.

data.title: Hollywood Bowl to Start $12.6 Mln Share Buyback Program
data.description:
By Ian Walker

Hollywood Bowl Group started a 10 million-pound ($12.6 million) share buyback program as approved by shareholders at the annual general meeting last month.

The bowling operator said it would start buying back the shares Tuesday and expects to complete the program by Jan. 29, 2026.

Hollywood Bowl said the buybacks reflect its cash-generative business model and strong balance sheet. It follows the previous share buyback announced in February 2024.

All shares purchased under the plan will be cancelled, the company said.

London-listed shares were up 8.0 pence, or 2.9%, at 281.0 pence in early trading. They are currently down 5.9% over the year to date and 12% lower over the past three months.

Write to Ian Walker at ian.walker@wsj.com

data.title: UK's Serica Energy falls as Triton platform shut due to Storm Eowyn-related damage
data.description:
** Shares of UK North Sea-focused oil and gas co Serica Energy fall 10.4% to 129p; among top pct losers across all London stocks

** Co suspends operations at its Triton offshore oil and gas platform in North Sea due to Storm Eowyn

** SQZ expects production from Triton to resume in mid-to-late March

** "Given that Triton FPSO was recovering strongly from operational issues of 2024, with material production from new wells, impact of Storm Eowyn is deeply frustrating," says CEO Chris Cox

** Co says its 2025 production outlook to be revised once it is clear on timeline and implications for necessary activities at Triton

** Stock fell 41.1% in 2024

data.title: Tencent Likely to Benefit From DeepSeek Integration — Market Talk
data.description: Tencent is likely to benefit from the integration of DeepSeek with its messaging app WeChat and its intelligent workstation product ima.copilot, Great Wall Securities analyst Hou Bin says in a research note. DeepSeek's open-source nature and cost efficiency have cut developing costs, and the collaboration between the two may become an example for the AI industry in the future, the analyst says. "As a domestic user ecosystem giant, [Tencent] has closely followed the AI ​​trend, and its overall ecosystem has gained momentum," the analyst says. Meanwhile, Tencent's game business has resumed an upward trend with growth gradually accelerating, and profitability has continued to improve, the analyst adds. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: U.K. Labor Market Is Set to Weaken — Market Talk
data.description: The U.K. jobs market is due some loosening, Deutsche Bank's Sanjay Raja tells investors in a note. Unemployment was stable at 4.4% at the end of last year and average pay growth picked up pace to 5.9%, figures show Tuesday, pointing to a robust labor market despite signs of weakness in separate surveys. But an increase in employer taxes due to come into effect this spring will come at a price, and pay growth will also likely slow following a spike from a rise in minimum wages also due this spring, Raja says. "The labor market is creaking but not cracking," he says. "Some slack is emerging, but we aren't seeing a worrying spiral in unemployment - just yet."(joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

data.title: Euro zone borrowing costs rise as investors see more defence spending ahead
data.description:
Longer-dated euro zone yields rose to two-week highs on Tuesday, extending the increases of the previous day when investors sold bonds on expectations that European governments would ramp up issuance to fund bigger defence spending.

German 10-year bond yield (DE10YT=RR), the benchmark for the euro zone bloc, rose 3 basis points (bps) to 2.51%, its highest since Jan. 31.

The euro zone benchmark rose 6 bps on Monday following remarks by European leaders that they would have to hike defence spending in response to the U.S. being less willing to take the lead on Europe's defence, likely requiring greater borrowing.

Eyes also remained on the Saudi capital, Riyadh, where U.S. and Russian officials are due to meet to discuss ending the Ukraine war. Europe may play a role in supporting Ukraine's security after any peace deal, underscoring the need for more military expenditure.

​ Italy's 10-year yield was last higher by 2 basis points​ at 3.57%. It touched 3.60% in early trade, also its highest level of the year.

Shorter-dated yields, which are more sensitive to expectations over central bank policy than long-term developments, moved less. Germany's two-year yield (DE2YT=RR) was about 1 bp higher at 2.15%.

data.title: Digital Silk Study Reveals Personalization and Customer Experience as Key Drivers of Brand Loyalty in 2025
data.description:
Los Angeles, California--(Newsfile Corp. - February 18, 2025) - Digital Silk, a leading digital agency specializing in strategic branding, web design, and digital marketing, highlights the growing importance of customer experience (CX) and personalization as the foundation for building brand loyalty in 2025. As digital interactions dominate consumer engagement, brands are increasingly focusing on creating tailored experiences that resonate with their audience.

Key Drivers of Brand Loyalty in 2025 - Image Digital Silk

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/10732/241185_b00d33bad4530a82_001full.jpg

In today's competitive market, consumers are not only seeking high-quality products but also seamless, personalized interactions. This underscores the critical role personalization plays in building long-term customer relationships.

Enhancing Customer Experience through Technology and Data

To stay ahead, brands are leveraging advanced technologies such as AI and machine learning to analyze customer behaviors and preferences in real time. The integration of these tools allows companies to deliver hyper-personalized experiences-whether through curated content, custom recommendations, or targeted campaigns.

At the end of the day, we as customers are increasingly expecting brands to understand us - our wants, needs, preferences, and behaviors. Brand-building is about relationship-building first and foremost, and technology helps us build that relationship faster, more accurately, and more effectively." said Courtney Bozigian, Director of Brand Strategy at Digital Silk.

Why Personalization Drives Brand Loyalty

  • Increased Engagement and Conversion: Personalized experiences lead to higher engagement rates, keeping customers invested in the brand's offerings.
  • Stronger Emotional Connection: Custom interactions create a deeper emotional connection, helping brands stand out in a crowded marketplace.
  • Enhanced Customer Retention: A focus on customer experience fosters loyalty and reduces churn. In fact, brands that prioritize CX see 1.6x higher customer satisfaction rates.

The Future of Personalization in 2025

As data-driven strategies continue to evolve, personalization will extend beyond traditional marketing to encompass the entire customer journey. From personalized website interfaces to AI-driven customer service solutions, companies that prioritize these innovations will gain a competitive edge.

For brands aiming to thrive in 2025, a focus on customer experience and personalization is no longer optional-it's essential. Those that invest in understanding their customers and delivering value at every touchpoint will be the leaders in building lasting brand loyalty.

About Digital Silk

Digital Silk is an award-winning California digital & brand agency dedicated to growing brands online. With a team of seasoned experts, Digital Silk delivers exceptional digital experiences through strategic branding, cutting-edge web design, and data-driven digital marketing services to boost customer engagement and increase conversions.

Contact Info:

Jessica Erasmus

Marketing Director & PR Manager

Tel: (800) 206-9413

Email: jessica@digitalsilk.com

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/241185

data.title: Ugandan shilling stable; importer FX demand low
data.description:
The Ugandan shilling was stable on Tuesday amid a slowdown in demand for dollars from merchandise importers, traders said.

At 0802 GMT, commercial banks quoted the shilling at 3,674/3,684, compared with Monday's closing rate of 3,675/3,685.

data.title: Valneva rises on positive surprise from 2025 topline, cash burn outlook
data.description:
** Stock in French vaccine producer Valneva rise more than 6% as 2025 outlook positively surprises following in-line 2024 revenue and cash figures

** Valneva eyes total revenues of between 180-190 million euros ($188.19-$198.65 million), with R&D spend between 90-100 million euros

** Van Lanschot Kempen flags "two positive surprises" in the outlook, with topline guidance ahead of estimates and cash burn to diminish by more than 50%,

** It paints a picture of "an increasingly healthy base business"

** Portzamparc notes a cushy cash flow, leaving the biotech company in a comfortable position to reach the next milestones of its clinical developments

** Valneva is among the best performers on France's wider SBF120 index

($1 = 0.9565 euros)

data.title: Valneva Expects Further Sales Growth
data.description:
By Helena Smolak

Valneva expects further sales growth this year after it posted higher revenue in 2024.

The French vaccine company said Tuesday that it expects total revenue in 2025 to grow to between 180 million euros and 190 million euros ($188.7 million and $199.2 million) with a positive cashflow. It expects to reach R&D investments of between 90 million and 100 million euros for this year.

Its full-year revenue rose 10% to 169.6 million euros despite lower-than anticipated sales of its attenuated chikungunya vaccine in the U.S.

Its cash and cash equivalents stood at 168.3 million euros as of Dec. 31.

The company will publish its audited full-year results on Mar. 20.

Write to Helena Smolak at helena.smolak@wsj.com

data.title: Market Chatter: HSBC Lays Off 40 Investment Bankers in Hong Kong
data.description:
HSBC Holdings cut 40 jobs at its investment bank in Hong Kong, Reuters reported Tuesday, citing people with knowledge on the matter.

The layoffs, which began on Monday, reportedly affected multiple investment banking sectors such as consumer, resources and energy, and mergers and acquisitions, among others.

The move is part of the British bank's restructuring plan to decrease costs.

HSBC did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Al Dhafra Insurance to Pause Trading in Abu Dhabi
data.description:
Al Dhafra Insurance's (ADX:DHAFRA) shares will temporarily halt trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange from 11 am local time on March 26 due to its annual general meeting.

The Abu Dhabi-listed insurance company will resume trading after it publishes its meeting results, according to a Tuesday release.
